source: EnterpriseRAG-Bench 'Conflicting Info' structure (near-duplicate, newer-supersedes-older) -- EXPANDED semantic eval set for the Bonsai zero-shot study (Phase 3c, fine-tune hinge).
license: MIT (upstream bench is MIT-licensed); specific titles/bodies are distilled fixtures crafted to probe the deterministic normalizer's ceiling vs Bonsai has_state.
purpose: Two questions: (1) OPPORTUNITY -- does Bonsai has_state catch paraphrased/semantic conflicts the deterministic normalizer (src/encoding/assertion_extractor.py) misses? (det catch-rate vs Bonsai catch-rate). (2) FINE-TUNE HINGE -- on the conflicts Bonsai catches, does decide_contradiction (zero-shot) adjudicate correctly (fix + supersede_assertion)? That quality gates whether the LoRA fine-tune on contradiction decision pairs is warranted.
det_normalizer_ceiling: The deterministic normalizer is LINE-ANCHORED and matches only ^key: value / ^key is/are/was [now] value / ^subject {chose|selected|adopted|picked|switched to|moved to|is now using|now uses|replaced X with} value. The 'paraphrased' pairs embed the state claim mid-sentence after a comma/clause or use a non-listed verb ('will be', 'is going to be', 'settled on', 'landed on', 'runs on', 'agreed ... will be') so the normalizer genuinely does NOT catch them. The harness MEASURES actual det-catch (self-validating) rather than trusting this label.
scoring: For each pair the harness extracts assertions from old+new bodies (deterministic normalizer vs Bonsai has_state), normalizes entities (src/encoding/assertion_extractor._norm_key), and checks for a COLLISION: a shared normalized entity with DIFFERENT values across the two docs. conflict=true pairs: a catch (collision) is a true positive (recall). conflict=false pairs: a catch is a false positive (precision). Adjudication runs only on caught conflict pairs: decide_contradiction should return fix + supersede_assertion (newer supersedes older).

old_doc.title: Deploy v1
old_doc.body:
# Deploy v1

deployment target is staging

new_doc.title: Deploy v2
new_doc.body:
# Deploy v2

deployment target is now production

old_doc.title: Team Standardization Notes
old_doc.body:
# Team Standardization Notes

We discussed the data layer and settled on MySQL going forward.

new_doc.title: Data Layer Update
new_doc.body:
# Data Layer Update

Going forward the team's data layer is Postgres.

pairs.miss_reason: Paraphrased, no line-anchored field/change-verb shape; apostrophe in 'team's' breaks the _FIELD_IS_RE key char class.

old_doc.title: Q2 Infra Notes
old_doc.body:
# Q2 Infra Notes

After the team meeting, we agreed that the database will be MySQL for the foreseeable future.

new_doc.title: Q3 Infra Update
new_doc.body:
# Q3 Infra Update

Looking ahead, the database is going to be Postgres across all services.

pairs.miss_reason: Leading clause + comma before the entity ('After the team meeting,'); 'will be' / 'is going to be' are not in the change-verb list.

old_doc.title: Launch Plan A
old_doc.body:
# Launch Plan A

Based on the architecture review, the deployment target is going to be staging for the first release.

new_doc.title: Launch Plan B
new_doc.body:
# Launch Plan B

Given the new timeline, the deployment target is going to be production, staging is no longer the plan.

pairs.miss_reason: Leading clause + comma; 'is going to be' not a listed change verb.

old_doc.title: Frontend Decision v1
old_doc.body:
# Frontend Decision v1

Following the spike, we agreed the framework will be React for the new app.

new_doc.title: Frontend Decision v2
new_doc.body:
# Frontend Decision v2

After re-evaluating, the framework is going to be Svelte going forward.

pairs.miss_reason: Leading clause + comma; 'will be' / 'is going to be' not listed change verbs.

old_doc.title: Cloud Choice v1
old_doc.body:
# Cloud Choice v1

In last week's review, we decided the hosting provider will be AWS.

new_doc.title: Cloud Choice v2
new_doc.body:
# Cloud Choice v2

Going forward, the hosting provider is going to be GCP for cost reasons.

pairs.miss_reason: Leading clause + comma; 'decided' / 'will be' / 'is going to be' not in the change-verb list.

old_doc.title: CI Notes v1
old_doc.body:
# CI Notes v1

Per the platform team, the CI runner will be GitHub Actions for all repos.

new_doc.title: CI Notes v2
new_doc.body:
# CI Notes v2

Moving ahead, the CI runner is going to be self-hosted Jenkins on our own metal.

pairs.miss_reason: Leading clause + comma; 'will be' / 'is going to be' not listed change verbs.

old_doc.title: Secrets v1
old_doc.body:
# Secrets v1

For the secrets store, the team landed on Vault as the backend.

new_doc.title: Secrets v2
new_doc.body:
# Secrets v2

These days, the secrets store runs on AWS Secrets Manager across the board.

pairs.miss_reason: Leading clause + comma; 'landed on' / 'runs on' not in the change-verb list. Also tests Bonsai entity-naming consistency ('secrets store' both docs).

old_doc.title: Tooling v1
old_doc.body:
# Tooling v1

After the discussion, we agreed the package manager will be npm.

new_doc.title: Tooling v2
new_doc.body:
# Tooling v2

Going forward, the package manager is going to be pnpm for the monorepo.

pairs.miss_reason: Leading clause + comma; 'will be' / 'is going to be' not listed change verbs.

old_doc.title: Region v1
old_doc.body:
# Region v1

For the primary deployment, the region is us-east-1 for latency reasons.

new_doc.title: Region v2
new_doc.body:
# Region v2

Now that most users are in Europe, the region is going to be eu-west-1 for everyone.

pairs.miss_reason: Leading clause + comma; 'is' sits after 'the region' but the line starts with 'For the primary deployment,' so _FIELD_IS_RE (line-anchored) does not match.

old_doc.title: January Status
old_doc.body:
# January Status

As of January, the build status was green on the old runner.

new_doc.title: July Status
new_doc.body:
# July Status

As of July, the build status is red on the new runner.

pairs.negatives_reason: Complementary temporal states (different dates) -- NOT a contradiction. A correct decider dismisses (or ask_user). Tests Bonsai precision: does it over-flag when provenance is temporally qualified?
Same entity, same value -- no collision (equal values), no contradiction. Tests that catch is defined as shared entity AND different value (not just shared entity).

old_doc.title: Frontend v1
old_doc.body:
# Frontend v1

The frontend framework will be React.

new_doc.title: Mobile v1
new_doc.body:
# Mobile v1

The mobile framework will be React.

pairs.negatives_reason: Different entities (frontend framework vs mobile framework), same value -- no shared entity, no contradiction. Tests Bonsai entity discrimination.
